STEAMe.title: ABM Device Lock
STEAMe.description: What It Is Activation Lock is an Apple security feature tied to iCloud/Apple ID that prevents a device from being erased and reactivated without the original Apple ID credentials. It kicks in when someone enables Find My on a device. Without a bypass, a locked device is a brick — you cannot enroll it, wipe it cleanly, or reassign it. What ABM Actually Changes This is where a lot of admins have a misconception worth addressing: ABM itself doesn't "turn on" Activation Lock. What ABM gives you (thr

STEAMe.title: Google Vault & Archive
STEAMe.description: Google Vault Google Vault is Google's native information governance and eDiscovery tool, built into Google Workspace. It allows administrators and legal teams to retain, search, and export data from Gmail, Google Drive, Chat, Meet recordings, and other Workspace services — even after a user has deleted that data from their own account. Vault is primarily used for three purposes: setting retention policies that define how long data is preserved before being purged, placing legal holds on specific

STEAMe.title: Slack message retention and DM retrieval
STEAMe.description: Slack stores messages on its servers and gives administrators varying levels of access to that data depending on the plan tier. Jackson Cano.title: ABM Research
Jackson Cano.description: What It Is Activation Lock is an Apple security feature tied to iCloud/Apple ID that prevents a device from being erased and reactivated without the original Apple ID credentials. It kicks in when someone enables Find My on a device. Without a bypass, a locked device is a brick — you cannot enroll it, wipe it cleanly, or reassign it.
